steps.title: Find the median of each data set
steps.content:
From the dot plots:
  • Data Set A has frequencies 1, 3, 4, 5, 4, 2, 1 at values 4–10 (20 values total)
  • Data Set B has frequencies 1, 2, 3, 4, 4, 3, 2 at values 4–10 (19 values total)

For Data Set A (20 values), the median is the average of the 10th and 11th values. The cumulative count through value 7 is 1+3+4+5 = 13, and through value 6 is 1+3+4 = 8. Both the 10th and 11th values fall at 7, so median A = 7.

For Data Set B (19 values), the median is the 10th value. The cumulative count through value 7 is 1+2+3+4 = 10, and through value 6 is 1+2+3 = 6. The 10th value falls at 7, so median B = 7.

Statement I is true.

steps.title: Compare standard deviations
steps.content:
Standard deviation measures how spread values are around the center.

Data Set A shows a sharp peak at value 7 (with 5 dots clustered at the center), while Data Set B has a flatter, more uniform distribution across the range 4–10.

A tightly concentrated distribution has smaller standard deviation; a widely dispersed distribution has larger standard deviation.

Since the two sets have different distributions, their standard deviations are different. Statement II is false. That matches choice B — B is correct.
